campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve several items including a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth sports and recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans for a new bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships
Extension of interim ordinance (temporary local rule)
New bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bike and pedestrian planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council introduced a new ordinance and decided to skip the usual first reading process to speed things up, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held - residents given opportunity to comment
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next council meeting
Fast-tracked approval process being used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026 to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the town manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services, though the specific details of these projects appear incomplete in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and closed session records
Reviewed city finances and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other contractors
Approved multi-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 22, 2026 to organize upcoming council business. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 28 meeting, made adjustments to the May 5 draft agenda, and reviewed public records from the previous week. The council also handled routine administrative tasks like scheduling specific times for agenda items when needed.
keyTopics: Finalized April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Reviewed and organized May 5, 2026 draft agenda
Set time schedules for specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026
Addressed notice and waiver requirements for agenda items

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and improvement projects will get funding. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting at 6 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, then reconvened at 7 P.M. for a regular public meeting that included recognizing award winners and other special honors.
keyTopics: Private closed session (details not disclosed to public)
Recognition and awards for winners
Special order business items
Regular council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 21, 2026 to discuss two ongoing legal cases that could potentially cost the city money, and to recognize the hard work of the Administrative Services Department (which handles budgeting, finances, and purchasing). The council also reserved time for residents to speak about any city issues they wanted to bring up.
keyTopics: Two legal cases with potential financial risk to the city
Recognition of the Administrative Services Department's budget and finance work
Public comment period for residents on any city matter

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract (2026-2029) between city and Local 1171 union
One agenda item was withdrawn by city staff

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to discuss several important financial and development matters. They held public hearings on a tax responsibility act, approved the budget and fees for fiscal year 2026-27, and reviewed a county plan to address homelessness in the area.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
User fees and service charges updates
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for the property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow protected trees to be cut down on this lot.
keyTopics: 751 Waverley Street tree removal permit under review
Protected trees at risk of being removed
Request to reconsider an already-approved permit decision
Planning Department reviewing their own permit approval
